author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Migration paused 2026-06-23. Surfaced during a Haiku-fan-out
attempt: `jj git init --colocate` + `jjf init` on a repo with
live source on `main` moves git HEAD to `refs/jj/root`,
wiping the working tree from git's point of view. Recovery
took manual `git symbolic-ref` + `git reset --hard` and lost
the operator's uncommitted edits in the asterinas-workspace
tree.

The bug is the SAME class as the `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` guard
on jjforge's own repo (`08cf14b`). The current storage choice
("files on a bookmark") forces a working-copy snapshot per
write, which in colocate moves git HEAD off `main`. The guard
is a workaround; for the asterinas tree, the workarounds are
(A) a permanent sibling clone, or (B) a real storage redesign
to out-of-tree refs (the git-bug pattern).

User picked B (research first, then re-attempt). Filed
`487536a storage-out-of-tree-refs` as a research ticket
under this epic + epic:mvp-storage. Adding `blocks` edge
from `487536a` to this ticket — migration won't ship until
the storage shape is decided.

The audit doc, the bug-fix work (four QA bugs closed
2026-06-23, tests 387 → 421), and the asterinas-side header
shape (961 .md files, ~96 epic dirs, header shape per
audit's mapping table) all carry over to the re-attempt.
No data lost from the planning side.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-23: Research ticket `487536a` closed. Verdict is to
move storage off the `issues` bookmark to per-issue git refs
(`refs/jjf/issues/<id>`), which kills HEAD drift at the source.
Design doc at `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`. Implementation
epic filed as `bd98097`.

This migration stays blocked on `bd98097` (v3 storage). The
research doc recommends waiting for v3 over shipping the
sibling-clone interim — the workaround would persist on every
read/write of asterinas data forever until v3, and the
asterinas markdown tree was already an archive long before the
migration attempt. ~3–4 sessions of v3 work; then re-attempt
the Haiku fan-out per `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md`.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Unblocked. `bd98097 storage-v3-out-of-tree`
shipped (8 tickets + 1 bug fix, ~3 sessions). jjforge now
writes to per-issue refs at `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` via
`git update-ref` CAS; git HEAD never moves on a mutation.
`jjf init` can run safely in any colocated jj+git repo,
including a repo with live source on `main`.

The HEAD-drift incident that paused this migration on
2026-06-23 is no longer reproducible — the substrate is
fixed. The Haiku fan-out plan in
`docs/host-asterinas-audit.md` should still apply
verbatim; only the "where does jjforge write" answer
changed and that's now invisible to the migrator code.

To re-attempt:
1. Fresh `git clone` or worktree of asterinas (NOT the
   one that already has `.jj/` — that has stale colocate
   state; either rm `.jj/` or use a fresh dir).
2. `jjf init` in the working tree. V3-shape plant — no
   bookmark, no working-copy mutation.
3. Dispatch the Haiku fan-out per the audit doc.

This is a fresh dispatch, not a continuation of the
2026-06-23 attempt.

author: opus-orchestrator <t@t.com>
body:
Trial migration of 2 epics complete (18 issues into
~/p/asterinas-workspace via fresh v3 init). The six prompt
tightenings the trial surfaced are folded into
`docs/host-asterinas-dispatch.md` (commit `086cc9b6`):

- Slug policy for sentinel filenames (PLAN/README/NOTES/TODO/SPEC).
- Phase-label rule covers textual prefixes (`phase:aarch64`,
  `phase:k3-framework13`) alongside numeric.
- Status default when `**Status:**` is missing (inherit parent
  epic, else `open`).
- Closed-blocker `Blocked by:` refs STILL create the edge
  (resolves trial-run 04e ambiguity).
- Body-cap split recipe references the new ticket `2542fdf`.
- Verification checklist with byte-fidelity spot-check the
  agent MUST complete before reporting done.

The dispatch doc includes a copy-paste prompt template at the
bottom — bulk-migration sessions pass that template + the slice
path. Reads-once-per-agent overhead in exchange for no
per-slice rule re-derivation.

Trial outputs preserved at
`~/p/jjforge/scratch/migration-report-{aarch64-qfl,host-net}.md`.

Migration epic stays open for the full bulk run; the trial
slices stay in `~/p/asterinas-workspace`'s `refs/jjf/issues/*`
as the seed.
